data.description:
## Summary
The three per-ticket gates run fine post-extraction but their rubrics are mutually inconsistent, their expected format is documented nowhere, and one still speaks dso dispatch language. Found while validating the gates against the 2026-06-09 audit tickets (43 tickets; substantive-but-unformatted bug tickets scored 1-3/fail while format-conformant prose passed).

## Findings (verified in source)
1. Rubric inconsistency: ticket-clarity-check.sh awards the Acceptance Criteria bonus ONLY for ticket_type=task (:185-188); for epics it rewards "## Success Criteria" (:215-218) — but check-acceptance-criteria.sh requires a literal "## Acceptance Criteria" heading with "- [" items on EVERY type (:60-66). So a clarity-perfect epic still fails check-ac unless it carries BOTH headings.
2. dso residue: issue-quality-check.sh fail messages say "using inline prompt" (:17,:123,:140,:50) — that described the dso sub-agent dispatcher's fallback, which does not exist in rebar.
3. Undocumented contract: the heading rubric (Reproduction Steps for bugs, Why/What for stories, Success Criteria+Context for epics, Acceptance Criteria checklists for all) appears only in gate source; README/CLAUDE.md never tell ticket authors (i.e. agents) the expected template, so gate failures are unactionable without reading gate code.
4. Honest-labeling: clarity-check is a structure/keyword heuristic (its header says "SC2 heuristic") — it measures format conformance, not semantic clarity; trivially satisfiable by headings + 500 filler chars. Acceptable as a floor check, but docs should say so.

## Fix direction
- Make the per-type rubrics consistent (decide: AC required for all types in clarity-check too, or check-ac learns per-type headings; recommend the former — one vocabulary).
- Replace "using inline prompt" with a rebar-appropriate reason string (update any asserting tests).
- Add a "ticket template" section to CLAUDE.md/README documenting the per-type headings the gates check, so agents author conformant tickets first time.
- Document the gates as structural floor checks (not semantic scoring) where they are described.

## Suggested RED test
Rubric-consistency test: a fixture ticket per type that passes clarity-check at threshold must also pass check-ac (RED under today's epic/story rubric divergence).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] One consistent heading vocabulary across clarity-check and check-ac (per-type matrix documented)
- [ ] No dso dispatch language in gate output (grep 'inline prompt' src/ clean)
- [ ] CLAUDE.md/README document the ticket template the gates enforce
- [ ] Existing gate tests updated; full suite green
data.title: Align quality-gate rubrics, remove dso dispatch language, document the ticket template
